volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 1.3.364 - Section 1.5.453)

sections.label: Section 1.3.364
sections.translation: Lawbreaker !

sections.label: Section 1.3.364b
sections.translation: Very much so .

sections.label: Section 1.3.364c
sections.translation: Pest of youth !

sections.label: Section 1.3.364d
sections.translation: Most severely said .

sections.label: Section 1.3.365
sections.translation: Thief !

sections.label: Section 1.3.365b
sections.translation: Oh ! wonderful !

sections.label: Section 1.3.365c
sections.translation: Vagabond !

sections.label: Section 1.3.365d
sections.translation: Pooh ! pooh [*] !

sections.label: Section 1.3.365e
sections.translation: Defrauder of the public !

sections.label: Section 1.3.365f
sections.translation: Most decidedly so .

sections.label: Section 1.3.366
sections.translation: Cheating scoundrel !

sections.label: Section 1.3.366b
sections.translation: Filthy

sections.label: Section 1.3.366c
sections.translation: pander !

sections.label: Section 1.3.366d
sections.translation: Lump of filth !

sections.label: Section 1.3.366e
sections.translation: A capital chorus .

sections.label: Section 1.3.367
sections.translation: You beat your father and mother .

sections.label: Section 1.3.367b
sections.translation: Aye , and killed them , too , rather than find them food ; did I do wrong at all ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.369
sections.original: In pertusum ingerimus dicta dolium , operam ludimus .
sections.translation: We are pouring our words into a pierced cask [*] : we are losing our pains .

sections.label: Section 1.3.370
sections.translation: Would you like to call me anything else besides ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.370b
sections.translation: Is there anything that shames you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.371
sections.original: Ten , amatorem esse inventum inanem quasi cassam nucem ?
sections.translation: Yes ; that you have been found to be a lover as empty as a rotten nut . But although you have used towards me expressions many and harsh , unless the Captain shall bring me this day the five minae that he owes me , as this was the last day appointed for the payment of that money ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.375
sections.original: si id non adfert , posse opinor facere me officium meum .
sections.translation: if he doesn ’ t bring it , I think that I am able to do my duty .

sections.label: Section 1.3.376
sections.translation: What is that duty ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.376b
sections.original: Si tu argentum attuleris , cum illo perdidero fidem :
sections.translation: If you bring the money , I ’ ll break faith with him ; that ’ s my duty . If it were more worth my while , I would talk further with you . But , without a coin of money , ’ tis in vain that you request me to have pity upon you . Such is my determination ; but do you , from this , consider what you have henceforth to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.380
sections.translation: Are you going then ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.380b
sections.translation: At present I am full of business .

sections.label: Section 1.3.380c
sections.translation: Before long you ’ ll be more so . That man is my own , unless all Gods and men forsake me . I ’ ll bone him just in the same fashion that a cook does a lamprey [*] . Now , Calidorus , I wish you to give me your attention .

sections.label: Section 1.3.383b
sections.translation: What do you bid me do ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.384
sections.original: Hoc ego oppidum admoenire , ut hodie capiatur , volo ;
sections.translation: I wish to lay siege to this town , that this day it may be taken .

sections.label: Section 1.3.385
sections.original: ad eam rem usust homine astuto , docto , cauto et callido ,
sections.translation: For that purpose , I have need of an artful , clever , knowing , and crafty fellow , who may despatch out of hand what he is ordered , not one to go to sleep upon his watch .

sections.label: Section 1.3.387
sections.translation: Tell me , then , what you are going to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.387b
sections.translation: In good time I ’ ll let you know . I don ’ t care for it to be repeated twice ; stories are made too long that way .

sections.label: Section 1.3.389
sections.translation: You plead what ’ s very fair and very just .

sections.label: Section 1.3.389b
sections.translation: Make haste ; bring the fellow hither quickly .

sections.label: Section 1.3.390
sections.original: Pauci ex multis sunt amici , hómini qui certi sient .
sections.translation: Out of many , there are but few friends that are to be depended upon by a person .

sections.label: Section 1.3.391
sections.original: Ego scio istuc . ergo utrumque , tibi nunc dilectum para
sections.translation: I know that ; therefore , get for yourself now a choice of both , and seek out of these many one that can be depended upon .

sections.label: Section 1.3.393
sections.translation: I ’ ll have him here this instant .

sections.label: Section 1.3.393b
sections.original: Potin ut abeas ? tibi moram dictis creas .
sections.translation: Can ’ t you be off then ? You create delay for yourself by your talking .

sections.label: Section 1.4.394
sections.original: postquam illic hinc abiit , tu astas solus , Pseudole .
sections.translation: Since he has gone hence , you are now standing alone , Pseudolus .

sections.label: Section 1.4.395
sections.original: quid nunc acturu ’ (s e) s , postquam erili filio
sections.translation: What are you to do now , after you have so largely promised costly delights to your master ’ s son by your speeches ? You , for whom not even one drop of sure counsel is ready , nor yet of silver * * * * nor have you where first you must begin your undertaking ,

sections.label: Section 1.4.400
sections.original: neque ad détexundam telam certos terminos .
sections.translation: nor yet fixed limits for finishing off your web . But just as the poet , when he has taken up his tablets , seeks what nowhere in the world exists , and still finds it , and makes that like truth which really is a fiction ; now I ’ ll become a poet ; twenty minae ,

sections.label: Section 1.4.405
sections.original: quae nusquam nunc sunt gentium , inveniam tamen .
sections.translation: which no-where in the world are now existing , still will I find . And some time since had I said that I would find them for him , and I had attempted to throw a net over our old gentleman ; however , by what means I know not , he perceived it beforehand . But my voice and my talking must be stopped ;

sections.label: Section 1.4.410
sections.original: erum eccum video húc Simonem una simul
sections.translation: for , see ! I perceive my master , Simo , coming this way , together with his neighbour , Callipho . Out of this old sepulchre will I dig twenty minae this day , to give them to my master ’ s son . Now I ’ ll step aside here , that I may pick up their conversation .

sections.label: Section 1.5.415
sections.original: Si de damnosis aut si dé amatoribus
sections.translation: If now a Dictator [*] were to be appointed at Athens of Attica out of the spendthrifts or out of the gallants , I do think that no one would surpass my son . For now the only talk of all throughout the city is to the effect that he is trying to set his mistress free ,

sections.label: Section 1.5.420
sections.original: argentum ad eam rem . hoc alii mihi renuntiant ;
sections.translation: and is seeking after money for that purpose . Some people bring me word of this ; and , in fact , I had long ago perceived it , and had suspected it , but I dissembled on it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.422b
sections.translation: Already is his son suspected by him ; this affair is nipt in the bud , this business is at a stand-still .

sections.label: Section 1.5.424
sections.translation: The way is now entirely blocked up against me , by which I had intended to go a-foraging for the money .

sections.label: Section 1.5.426
sections.translation: He has perceived it beforehand . There ’ s no booty for the marauders .

sections.label: Section 1.5.427
sections.original: Homines qui gestant quique auscultant crimina ,
sections.translation: Those men who carry about and who listen to accusations , should all be hanged , if so it could be at my decision , the carriers by their tongues , the listeners by their ears .

sections.label: Section 1.5.430
sections.original: nam istaec quae tibi renuntiantur , filium
sections.translation: For these things that are told you , that your son in his amour is desirous to chouse you out of money , the chance is that these things so told you are all lies . But sappose they are true , as habits are , now-a-days especially , what has he done so surprising ? What new thing , if a young man

sections.label: Section 1.5.435
sections.translation: does love , and if he does liberate his mistress ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.435b
sections.translation: A delightful old gentleman .

sections.label: Section 1.5.436
sections.translation: I don ’ t wish him to follow the old-fashioned habits [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.5.436b
sections.translation: But still , in vain do you object ; or you yourself shouldn ’ t have done the like in your youthful days . It befits the father to be immaculate , who wishes his son to be more immaculate than he has been himself .

sections.label: Section 1.5.440
sections.original: nam tu quod damni et quod fecisti flagiti
sections.translation: But the mischief and the profligacy you were guilty of might have been distributed throughout the whole population , a share for each man . Are you surprised at it , if the son does take after the father ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.443
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , quam pauci éstis homines commodi . em ,
sections.translation: O Zeus , Zeus [*] ! how few in number are you considerate men . See , that ’ s being a father to a son , just as is proper .

sections.label: Section 1.5.445
sections.original: Quis hic lóquitur ? meus est hic quidem sérvos Pseudolus .
sections.translation: Who is it that ’ s speaking here ? ( Looking round .) Why , surely ’ tis my servant Pseudolus . ’ Tis he corrupts my son , the wicked scoundrel ; he is his leader , he his tutor . I long for him to be put to extreme torture .

sections.label: Section 1.5.448b
sections.translation: This is folly now , thus to keep your anger in readiness . How much better were it

sections.label: Section 1.5.450
sections.translation: to accost him with kind words and to make all enquiries , whether these things are true or not that they tell you of ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.453
sections.translation: I ’ ll take your advice .
